scenes.title: Meeting Someone
scenes.narrative: You walk into a cozy warung in Ubud. The owner smiles warmly and greets you. Time to introduce yourself and make your first connection in Indonesia.

scenes.title: Finding Your Way
scenes.narrative: You're standing at a crossroads near a busy market in Yogyakarta. You need to find the kraton (palace) but the streets are winding. Time to ask for directions.

scenes.title: Getting Food
scenes.narrative: The aroma of sate and nasi goreng fills the air as you sit down at a bustling street food stall. The cook looks at you expectantly — what will you order?

scenes.title: Shopping
scenes.narrative: You wander through a vibrant market in Bali. Colorful batik fabrics hang everywhere, wooden carvings line the stalls. The vendor waves you over — let's haggle!

scenes.title: Emergency
scenes.narrative: Something's gone wrong — maybe you've lost your bag or you're not feeling well. You need help fast. These are the words that could save the day.

scenes.title: At the Hotel
scenes.narrative: You arrive at your guesthouse in Seminyak after a long flight. The receptionist greets you with a warm smile. Time to check in and get settled.

scenes.title: Small Talk
scenes.narrative: You're sitting on a shared bemo ride in Bali and the person next to you starts chatting. Keep the conversation flowing with these handy words.

scenes.title: Time & Numbers
scenes.narrative: The shopkeeper holds up fingers as he counts out your change. The bus leaves at a certain hour. Numbers and time are everywhere — here are the essentials.

scenes.title: Getting Around
scenes.narrative: Bali's traffic is legendary. You need to catch a taxi, find the bus, or maybe just walk. Here's how to get from A to B.

scenes.title: Being Polite
scenes.narrative: Indonesians are famously warm and polite. A few well-placed courtesy words go a long way — and will earn you instant smiles.

words.sample_sentence: Maaf, saya tidak mengerti bahasa Indonesia.

scenes.title: Basic Verbs
scenes.narrative: Actions are the engine of any language. These core verbs pop up constantly in daily Indonesian life — from asking to giving to knowing.

scenes.title: Pronouns & Connectors
scenes.narrative: These tiny words are the glue that holds sentences together. Without them, you'd sound like a caveman — but with them, you can start building real thoughts.

scenes.title: Common Adjectives
scenes.narrative: You want to describe the giant temple you saw, the tiny gecko on the wall, or the beautiful sunset. These adjectives let you paint with words.

scenes.title: Yes, No & Maybe
scenes.narrative: The simplest responses can be the most powerful. Master these and you can navigate any yes-or-no situation with confidence.

scenes.title: People & Family
scenes.narrative: Indonesians love talking about family. Whether you're meeting someone's kids or explaining your own family back home, these words come up constantly.

scenes.title: Food & Drink Essentials
scenes.narrative: Beyond the basics — you need to know your ayam from your ikan, your teh from your kopi. This is the vocabulary of Indonesian culinary life.

scenes.title: Essential Questions
scenes.narrative: Questions are your most powerful tool as a traveler. Every one of these opens a door to understanding — and shows people you're genuinely trying.

scenes.title: Greetings & Farewells
scenes.narrative: Every encounter begins and ends with these phrases. Indonesians will light up when they hear you use the right greeting for the right time of day.

scenes.title: Feelings & Emotions
scenes.narrative: Your Indonesian friend asks how you're really doing. Beyond 'baik,' here's how to express what you're actually feeling inside.

scenes.title: Opinions & Thoughts
scenes.narrative: Now you can share what you think — about the food, the weather, the view. Indonesians love hearing visitors' opinions about their beautiful country.

scenes.title: Making Plans
scenes.narrative: Your new friends want to hang out tomorrow. Let's make plans — when, where, and what you'll do together.

scenes.title: Describing Things
scenes.narrative: The temple is huge. The monkey is tiny. The sunset is indescribable. Expand your ability to describe the world around you.

scenes.title: At Work
scenes.narrative: Maybe you're a digital nomad in Canggu, or chatting with a local about their job. Here's work vocabulary that comes up in everyday conversation.

scenes.title: Technology & Internet
scenes.narrative: Finding WiFi in Indonesia is a daily quest. Whether you're looking for a connection or trying to order a Grab, these words are essential for modern travelers.

scenes.title: Health & Body
scenes.narrative: Bali belly, sunburn, or just a headache — knowing body parts and health words lets you get the help you need at a pharmacy or clinic.

scenes.title: Nature & Weather
scenes.narrative: Indonesia's natural beauty is breathtaking — from volcanoes to rice terraces. Here's how to talk about the incredible world around you.

scenes.title: Entertainment
scenes.narrative: Time to relax! Whether you're watching a show, playing games, or listening to music, here's your entertainment vocabulary.

scenes.title: Relationships
scenes.narrative: From family dynamics to new friendships, Indonesian social life revolves around connections. These words help you navigate the web of relationships.

scenes.title: Daily Routine
scenes.narrative: From waking up to going to bed, your day has a rhythm. Here are the words that describe the flow of a typical Indonesian day.

scenes.title: Home & Living
scenes.narrative: Whether you're renting a villa in Canggu or staying with a host family, these words help you talk about the place you call home.

scenes.title: Education & Learning
scenes.narrative: You're learning Indonesian right now! Here are words about school, studying, and the beautiful process of learning itself.

scenes.title: Colors
scenes.narrative: From the deep blue of the ocean to the vibrant green of the rice terraces, Indonesia is a feast of color. Name what you see.

scenes.title: More Numbers
scenes.narrative: Beyond one, two, three — you need bigger numbers for prices, addresses, and phone numbers. Indonesian numbers are beautifully logical.

scenes.title: Clothing & Appearance
scenes.narrative: Shopping for batik? Describing what someone's wearing? From sarongs to sandals, here's your fashion vocabulary.

scenes.title: Giving Directions
scenes.narrative: Now that you know how to ask for directions, it's time to learn to give them — and understand more detailed instructions from locals.

scenes.title: Transportation
scenes.narrative: Buses, motorbikes, ferries, and planes — Indonesia is a country of islands and you'll use them all. Here's how to talk about getting around.

scenes.title: Quantity & Size
scenes.narrative: More rice? Less sugar? A little bit of sambal? These words help you fine-tune exactly how much of something you want.

scenes.title: Animals
scenes.narrative: From cheeky monkeys at temples to geckos on the ceiling, Indonesian wildlife is everywhere. Name the creatures you encounter.

scenes.title: Days of the Week
scenes.narrative: Planning your itinerary? Temples are closed on certain days, markets happen weekly. Know your hari Senin from hari Jumat.

scenes.title: Cooking & Kitchen
scenes.narrative: You join a cooking class in Ubud and learn to make rendang. The chef tosses ingredients and barks instructions — keep up!

scenes.title: Feelings About Places
scenes.narrative: Indonesia's temples, beaches, and jungles inspire awe. Here's how to express how a place makes you feel.

scenes.title: Verbs of Motion
scenes.narrative: Running, swimming, climbing — Indonesia is an adventure and these action words keep you moving through it.

scenes.title: Weather & Seasons
scenes.narrative: Indonesia has two seasons — wet and dry. The weather shapes everything from travel plans to daily life. Here's how to talk about it.

scenes.title: Money & Banking
scenes.narrative: Rupiah notes come in the millions, ATMs are everywhere, and bargaining is an art. Navigate the financial side of Indonesia.

scenes.title: Communication Verbs
scenes.narrative: Talking, writing, reading, listening — the acts of communication themselves. Essential for saying what you need to say about saying things.

scenes.title: Religion & Culture
scenes.narrative: Indonesia is deeply spiritual. From Hindu temples in Bali to mosques in Java, understanding cultural vocabulary shows deep respect.

scenes.title: More Useful Verbs
scenes.narrative: Expand your action repertoire. These verbs pop up in everyday conversations and will make you sound much more fluent.

scenes.title: Time Expressions
scenes.narrative: Indonesian time culture is famously relaxed — 'jam karet' (rubber time). But you still need to know how to talk about when things happen.

scenes.title: Shopping & Markets
scenes.narrative: Indonesia's markets are an experience. From art markets to night markets to supermarkets, here's your expanded shopping vocabulary.

scenes.title: Restaurant & Ordering
scenes.narrative: Beyond basic food words — how to navigate a menu, make requests, and handle the bill like a local.

scenes.title: Household Verbs
scenes.narrative: Daily life at your homestay or villa involves cleaning, fixing, and managing the space. These verbs cover the domestic basics.

scenes.title: Body & Senses
scenes.narrative: Your five senses guide you through Indonesia — the smells of spices, the sounds of gamelan, the sight of rice terraces.

scenes.title: Abstract Concepts
scenes.narrative: As your Indonesian grows, you can express bigger ideas — time, life, truth. These abstract words elevate your conversations.

scenes.title: Verbs of Possession & Exchange
scenes.narrative: Giving, receiving, sharing, borrowing — the verbs of exchange that grease the wheels of human interaction.

scenes.title: Expressing Needs
scenes.narrative: Sometimes you need something urgently — medicine, a bathroom, directions. Here's how to express what you need right now.

scenes.title: Places in Town
scenes.narrative: Navigating a new Indonesian town? You need to know where things are — the bank, the hospital, the temple, the beach.

scenes.title: Comparisons & Superlatives
scenes.narrative: Comparing things is how we make sense of the world — which is bigger, better, cheaper? Indonesian makes this wonderfully simple.

scenes.title: Polite Requests & Responses
scenes.narrative: Level up your politeness game. Indonesians deeply appreciate respectful communication, and these phrases show cultural awareness.

scenes.title: Indonesian Slang & Informal
scenes.narrative: Hanging out at a kopi shop in Jakarta, your Indonesian friends switch to slang. Textbooks won't teach you these — but every local uses them daily.

scenes.title: Food Preparation
scenes.narrative: You're deeper into your Ubud cooking class now. The chef demonstrates advanced techniques — grilling, steaming, slicing. Keep up with the culinary action.

scenes.title: Geography
scenes.narrative: Indonesia is the world's largest archipelago — over 17,000 islands! From rivers cutting through jungles to volcanic lakes, the geography is stunning.

scenes.title: Sports & Exercise
scenes.narrative: Your Balinese friend invites you to play futsal after work. Staying active in Indonesia means knowing the sports vocabulary — from swimming to the gym.

words.sample_sentence: Kita main sepak bola sore ini.

scenes.title: Music & Art
scenes.narrative: The sound of gamelan drifts from a temple ceremony. A painter captures the rice terraces in watercolor. Indonesia's art scene is vibrant and deeply rooted.

scenes.title: Social Media
scenes.narrative: Everyone in Indonesia is on social media — Instagram, TikTok, WhatsApp. Your new friends want to connect online. Here's the digital vocabulary.

words.sample_sentence: Berbagi cerita perjalanan di media sosial.

scenes.title: Business
scenes.narrative: You meet a local entrepreneur at a co-working space in Canggu. Business is booming in Indonesia, and these words let you talk shop.

scenes.title: Legal & Official
scenes.narrative: Extending your visa, signing a rental agreement, or registering at the local office — bureaucracy in Indonesia requires its own vocabulary.

scenes.title: Emotions Advanced
scenes.narrative: Beyond happy and sad — now you can express the full range of human emotion. Frustration at traffic, pride in your language progress, anxiety before a flight.

words.sample_sentence: Saya bangga bisa bicara bahasa Indonesia.

scenes.title: Household Items
scenes.narrative: Settling into your rental in Bali, you realize you need to know what things are called. The wardrobe won't close, the fan is broken, you need an extra pillow.

scenes.title: Gardening & Plants
scenes.narrative: Your Balinese host tends a lush tropical garden every morning. Frangipani, orchids, and herbs grow everywhere. She teaches you the names.

words.sample_sentence: Tanaman ini perlu air setiap hari.

scenes.title: Parties & Celebrations
scenes.narrative: Your Indonesian friend is turning 25 and throws a big party. Birthday celebrations, weddings, and festivals are joyful social occasions here.

scenes.title: Marine Life
scenes.narrative: Snorkeling in the Gili Islands, you see a sea turtle gliding past coral. Indonesia has some of the richest marine biodiversity on Earth.

words.sample_sentence: Terumbu karang di sini sangat indah.

scenes.title: Coffee Culture
scenes.narrative: Indonesia is one of the world's top coffee producers. From thick kopi tubruk to trendy third-wave cafes, coffee culture runs deep here.

scenes.title: Textiles & Crafts
scenes.narrative: You visit a traditional weaving village in Flores. Skilled artisans create intricate ikat textiles, silver jewelry, and wood carvings by hand.

scenes.title: Daily Errands
scenes.narrative: Living in Indonesia means running errands in Bahasa. Queuing at the post office, topping up your phone, doing laundry — the mundane but essential tasks.

words.sample_sentence: Saya mau kirim paket ke Jakarta.

scenes.title: Indonesian Cuisine
scenes.narrative: Indonesia's culinary heritage spans hundreds of ethnic groups. From rendang to gudeg, each dish tells a story of the region it comes from.

scenes.title: Environmental
scenes.narrative: Indonesia faces big environmental challenges — from plastic in the oceans to deforestation. Many locals are passionate about sustainability.

scenes.title: Feelings About People
scenes.narrative: Describing someone's character goes beyond just 'baik.' Your Indonesian friends are generous, honest, or sometimes a bit stingy. Here's how to say it.

scenes.title: Travel Logistics
scenes.narrative: Booking flights, confirming reservations, dealing with cancellations — the practical side of travel requires its own vocabulary.

situations.title: At the Airport
situations.narrative: You've just landed at Ngurah Rai Airport. The warm tropical air hits you as you step off the plane. Navigate arrivals, customs, and your ride to the hotel.

situations.title: Getting Around Bali
situations.narrative: Bali has no trains and limited public transport. You'll rely on ojeks, Grabs, and maybe your own scooter. Here's how to navigate.

situations.title: At Restaurants & Warungs
situations.narrative: From beachside fine dining to a plastic-stool warung, Bali's food scene is incredible. Order with confidence and discover local specialties.

words.sample_sentence: Babi guling di Ibu Oka terkenal.

situations.title: At the Hotel / Villa
situations.narrative: Your Bali villa has a private pool and the staff is incredibly attentive. Here's how to communicate your needs and requests.

situations.title: Shopping & Bargaining
situations.narrative: Bali's art markets are a haggling paradise. Kuta, Ubud, Sukawati — each has treasures if you know how to negotiate.

situations.title: Sightseeing & Temples
situations.narrative: Bali is the Island of the Gods. Ancient temples, rice terraces, and sacred springs await. Show respect and enjoy the spiritual beauty.

situations.title: Emergency in Bali
situations.narrative: You hope you won't need these, but just in case — whether it's a motorbike scrape, lost belongings, or an allergic reaction, be prepared.

words.sample_sentence: Luka di kaki saya perlu diobati.
